content.Text: [Wed 2026-04-22 08:08 PDT] Create a new interactive HTML tool at `docs/partitioning.html` for **Graph Partitioning** in this GraphVisual project. ## What to build An interactive graph partitioning tool with: 1. **3 Algorithms:** - **Kernighan-Lin (KL)** bisection — iterative swap-based improvement - **Spectral Bisection** — Fiedler vector (2nd smallest eigenvalue of Laplacian) - **Multi-level k-way** — recursive bisection for k>2 partitions 2. **Interactive Canvas visualization:** - Force-directed layout with nodes colored by partition - Drag nodes between partitions manually - Cut edges highlighted in red/dashed - Partition stats: cut size, balance ratio, edge cut percentage 3. **8 Preset graphs:** Karate Club, Grid 4x4, Random (20 nodes), Barbell, Two Cliques, Wheel, Petersen, Custom (text input) 4. **Proactive recommendations:** - Detect unbalanced partitions → suggest rebalancing - Detect high cut ratio → suggest different algorithm - Detect disconnected partitions → warn user - Suggest optimal k based on modularity 5. **Controls panel (left sidebar):** - Algorithm selector dropdown - Number of partitions (k) slider (2-8) - Preset selector - Run button, Reset button - Text input for custom graphs (adjacency list format) - Animation speed slider - Auto-optimize toggle (runs KL improvement passes automatically) 6. **Metrics dashboard:** - Cut size (number of cross-partition edges) - Balance ratio (min partition / max partition) - Modularity score - Edge cut percentage - Per-partition node count 7. **Step-by-step animation** for KL algorithm showing swap decisions 8. **Export:** JSON export of partition assignment ## Style Requirements Follow the exact same style as other tools in `docs/`. Use: - `` for base styles - CSS variables: `var(--surface)`, `var(--border)`, `var(--accent)`, `var(--bg)`, `var(--text)`, `var(--text-muted)`, `var(--accent-hover)`, `var(--surface-hover)` - Same sidebar navigation as other pages (copy from `docs/resilience.html` sidebar) - Grid layout: 300px controls panel left, canvas right - All self-contained in one HTML file (inline JS + inline CSS beyond styles.css) ## Sidebar Navigation Copy the full sidebar from `docs/resilience.html` but: - Add this new tool: `✂️Partitioning` - Mark it as active, unmark all others ## Also update `docs/index.html` Add the partitioning tool link in the sidebar navigation (after link-predictor or wherever tools section ends): ```html ✂️Partitioning ``` ## Build verification After creating the file, run: `python -c "import html.parser; html.parser.HTMLParser().feed(open('docs/partitioning.html').read()); print('HTML OK')"` to verify the HTML parses. Do NOT create any branches or PRs. Just make the changes to the working directory files.
I’m inspecting the existing `docs` pages first so the new tool matches the project’s current layout, sidebar, and styling conventions before I add `partitioning.html` and update `index.html`.

I’ve got the sidebar and page structure. Next I’m checking `styles.css` and one comparable interactive tool so the new page uses the same layout primitives and doesn’t drift from the docs’ existing UI.

I have enough context. I’m going to add a self-contained `docs/partitioning.html` that follows the existing docs tool pattern, with force-layout visualization, KL/spectral/multilevel partitioning, recommendations, metrics, export, and the resilience-style sidebar. Then I’ll patch `docs/index.html` and run the requested HTML parse check.

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

🏝️

Independent Set Finder

Find maximum independent sets with greedy, exact backtracking, and complement-clique algorithms. Step-by-step animation with Gallai theorem verification.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.
🏗️

Architecture

Project structure, data pipeline, algorithm details, component design.
🗄️

Database Schema

PostgreSQL table schemas, query patterns, data pipeline ordering.
📖

API Reference

Detailed class and method documentation for all public APIs.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

🏝️

Independent Set Finder

Find maximum independent sets with greedy, exact backtracking, and complement-clique algorithms. Step-by-step animation with Gallai theorem verification.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment
